categories.name: Software Engineering & Testing
categories.description: These articles serve as my understanding of software testing. They are based on the slides from the software testing lecture when I was a student at LMU.

articles.title: A Guide to Black-Box Testing: Equivalence, Boundary, and Decision Table Techniques
articles.summary: A look at black-box testing, a method where test cases are derived from the external specification without considering the internal code structure.

articles.title: The Test Oracle Problem: How Do You Know Your Test *Really* Passed?
articles.summary: A test input is only half of a test case. The other half is the oracle. This post explores the challenge of determining the correct output for a given test.

articles.title: Measuring Test Suite Quality: A Guide to Coverage Criteria and Mutation Testing
articles.summary: How do you assess the quality of a test suite? This post covers adequacy criteria, from statement and branch coverage to mutation testing.

articles.title: An Introduction to White-Box Testing: Symbolic Execution and Search-Based Testing
articles.summary: An exploration of white-box testing, where the internal structure of the program is used to derive test inputs, focusing on symbolic execution and SBST.

articles.title: Managing Evolving Code: A Guide to Regression Testing
articles.summary: Software is not static. This post covers the techniques for regression testing: minimization, selection, and prioritization.

articles.title: A Look at Concolic Execution: The Hybrid of Concrete and Symbolic Testing
articles.summary: Concolic execution (CONCrete + symbOLIC) is a hybrid approach that addresses the practical limitations of pure symbolic execution.

categories.name: Cybersecurity & Machine Learning
categories.description: Explorations into cybersecurity concepts and machine learning applications.

articles.title: From Random Guesses to Intelligent Grammars: A Look at Fuzzing and Random Testing
articles.summary: A look at random testing and fuzzing, from simple mutational fuzzers to advanced grammar-based fuzzing for generating syntactically valid inputs.

categories.name: Machine Learning
categories.description: This category reflects my understanding of Machine Learning based on the lecture I attended at LMU.

articles.title: An Introduction to Machine Learning: A Journey Through Its History and Ideas
articles.summary: A high-level introduction to machine learning, its philosophical underpinnings, and its historical development from early statistical methods to modern deep learning.

articles.title: The Perceptron: A Glimpse into the Dawn of Machine Learning
articles.summary: An introduction to the Perceptron algorithm, one of the earliest and most foundational models in machine learning, covering its architecture, learning rule, and historical significance.

articles.title: A Refresher on Linear Algebra: The Language of Machine Learning
articles.summary: A review of the fundamental concepts of linear algebra—vectors, matrices, and their operations—that form the mathematical language of machine learning.

articles.title: Linear Regression: The Workhorse of Machine Learning
articles.summary: An in-depth look at linear regression, from the method of least squares to the importance of regularization and its role in understanding causality.

articles.title: Basis Functions: Transforming Non-Linear Problems into Linear Ones
articles.summary: An exploration of basis functions and their role in transforming non-linear problems into linear ones, enabling the use of linear models for complex tasks.

articles.title: Understanding Function Approximation and the Curse of Dimensionality in Machine Learning
articles.summary: An exploration of function approximation as a framework for understanding machine learning and the critical challenge of the Curse of Dimensionality.

articles.title: Kernel Methods: The Power of Infinite Dimensions and the Kernel Trick
articles.summary: An exploration of kernel methods, their relationship to basis functions, and their ability to handle high-dimensional data through the kernel trick.

articles.title: A Refresher on Probability: The Foundation of Uncertainty in Machine Learning
articles.summary: A review of the fundamental concepts of probability theory—from random variables to Bayes' theorem—that are essential for understanding machine learning.

articles.title: Frequentist vs. Bayesian Statistics: Two Perspectives on Inference and Learning
articles.summary: An exploration of the philosophical and practical differences between Frequentist and Bayesian statistics, and their implications for machine learning.

articles.title: An Introduction to Neural Networks: The Power of Learned Representations
articles.summary: An introduction to neural networks, covering their architecture, the backpropagation algorithm, and key concepts like regularization.

articles.title: An Introduction to Linear Classifiers: Drawing the Line in Data
articles.summary: An exploration of linear classifiers and the different approaches to building them, including generative models, logistic regression, and classification via regression.

articles.title: Support Vector Machines: Finding the Optimal Margin
articles.summary: An introduction to the Support Vector Machine (SVM), a powerful classification algorithm based on the principle of maximizing the margin between classes.

articles.title: Model Selection and Estimating Generalization Performance
articles.summary: An overview of model selection, the estimation of generalization error, and the theoretical frameworks that guide the process.

articles.title: An Introduction to Deep Learning: From Revival to Revolution
articles.summary: An overview of the fundamental principles of deep learning, exploring the key ingredients that contribute to its success and the theoretical concepts that explain its power.

articles.title: Modeling Sequential Data: From Recurrent Neural Networks to the Attention Revolution
articles.summary: An exploration of architectures designed for sequential data, from Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) to the powerful attention mechanisms that underpin modern models like the Transformer.

articles.title: Modeling Sequential Data: From Recurrent Neural Networks to the Attention Revolution
articles.summary: An exploration of architectures designed for sequential data, from Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) to the powerful attention mechanisms that underpin modern models like the Transformer.

articles.title: Manifold Learning, Autoencoders, and Generative Models: Uncovering the Intrinsic Structure of Data
articles.summary: An exploration of manifold learning, autoencoders, and generative models, and how they are used to uncover the intrinsic structure of data.

categories.name: Linear Algebra for Machine Learning
categories.description: Personal notes covering foundational linear algebra concepts for machine learning. These notes aim to help others understand the mathematical foundations of modern ML algorithms.

articles.title: Linear Algebra for Machine Learning: Introduction
articles.summary: An introduction to these notes, prerequisites, and the fundamental role of linear algebra in machine learning algorithms.

articles.title: Vectors and Vector Spaces
articles.summary: Exploring vectors as ordered lists of numbers, vector space axioms, linear combinations, and their applications in feature representation and embeddings.

articles.title: Matrices and Data Representation
articles.summary: Understanding matrices as rectangular arrays of numbers, matrix operations, special matrices, and their use in representing datasets and transformations.

articles.title: Dot Products and Vector Norms
articles.summary: Examining inner products for measuring similarity, vector norms (L1, L2, L-infinity) for quantifying magnitude, and their roles in distance metrics and regularization.

articles.title: Matrix Multiplication and Linear Transformations
articles.summary: Covering matrix-vector and matrix-matrix multiplication, geometric interpretations as linear transformations, and applications in neural network layers.

articles.title: Systems of Linear Equations
articles.summary: Formulating and solving systems of linear equations using Gaussian elimination, understanding solution types, and applications to regression problems.

articles.title: Matrix Inverse, Linear Independence, and Rank
articles.summary: Defining matrix inverses and their computation, exploring linear independence and rank, and their implications for multicollinearity and matrix factorization.

articles.title: Eigenvectors and Eigenvalues
articles.summary: Understanding eigenvectors as special directions preserved under transformation, computing eigenvalues via the characteristic equation, and applications in stability analysis.

articles.title: Eigendecomposition of a Matrix
articles.summary: Decomposing matrices into eigenvalues and eigenvectors, computing matrix powers and functions efficiently, and applications in PCA and spectral methods.

articles.title: Singular Value Decomposition (SVD)
articles.summary: Factorizing any matrix into orthogonal and diagonal components, low-rank approximations, pseudo-inverse computation, and applications in recommender systems.

articles.title: Application: Principal Component Analysis (PCA)
articles.summary: Applying eigendecomposition to reduce dimensionality while preserving variance, implementing PCA via covariance matrices and SVD, and use cases in visualization and feature extraction.

articles.title: Vector Projections and Orthogonality
articles.summary: Projecting vectors onto subspaces, understanding orthogonal and orthonormal bases, Gram-Schmidt orthogonalization, and applications in QR decomposition.

articles.title: Application: Linear Regression and the Normal Equation
articles.summary: Deriving the closed-form solution for linear regression, geometric interpretation as projection, computational methods, and ridge regularization.

articles.title: Matrix Calculus: Gradients with Vectors and Matrices
articles.summary: Computing gradients of scalar functions with respect to vectors and matrices, chain rule for backpropagation, common derivatives, and applications in optimization.

articles.title: Review and Next Steps
articles.summary: Summarizing the key concepts from these notes, connecting linear algebra to machine learning algorithms, and providing recommendations for further study and practical implementation.
